news.title: The Football Boots That Defined the 2026 World Cup
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is over, which means we’ll have to find something else to do until the regular season starts up again. The only upside to the tournament’s conclusion is that we can look back through the boots that defined this epic, 104-match event. The group stages saw every big footballing powerhouse debut new boots in pink, including Nike’s ‘Breakout’ Pack, Team Trefoil’s ‘Road to Glory’ Pack, and PUMA’s ‘Showtime’ Pack. When the knockout stages arrived, many players switched from pink-adorned boots to other specialised iterations, like Team Swoosh’s ‘Scorpion’ Mercurials and Messi’s signature adidas F50.

news.title: The US should never host another tournament after World Cup debacle
news.lede: The moral, financial and logistical issues that plagued this World Cup should be enough to disqualify the US from hosting major football tournaments until serious changes are made, argues Richard Jolly. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' results
news.lede: Look back at all the European sides' results at the FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concluded with Spain's extra-time final victory against holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 3-1 vs Jordan (San Francisco Bay Area) Monday 22 June: L 0-2 vs Argentina (Dallas) Sunday 28 June: D 3-3 vs Algeria (Kansas City) Thursday 2 July (R32): L 3-0 vs Spain (Los Angeles).

news.title: World Cup Golden Boot: Kylian Mbappé wins after Lionel Messi is held scoreless in final
news.lede: The stars were out throughout the 2026 World Cup, and they shined bright through the final days of the tournament. France's Kylian Mbappé was the last player to move out in front of the Golden Boot race, striking twice in Les Bleus' third-place match against England after they had stumbled to a 4-0 deficit in the first half Saturday. That put him two goals ahead of Lionel Messi before Argentina took the pitch in Sunday's final against Spain. Mbappé held on to win the Golden Boot, the award bestowed upon the World Cup's top goal scorer, after Messi was held scoreless in Argentina's 1-0 extra-time loss to Spain.

news.title: Eight things we learned from Spain’s 1-0 victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final
news.lede: In yet another battle of attrition for this World Cup, Spain ousted the defending champs, Argentina, 1-0 in a tense, physical, and sometimes sloppy finale. An extra time banger from Ferran Torres was the difference, but it was much deeper than a timely goal. Argentina’s mastery of referees and the clock betrayed them on the biggest stage, while Spain’s consistency was the difference. Here are some things we learned from the World Cup finale.

news.title: Spanish Football Fans Celebrate Second World Cup Victory
news.lede: Spanish team at the Cibeles Square on Monday. Two million people, according to Madrid government data, poured into the capital’s avenues during Monday afternoon to welcome the champions, who toured the city on an open-top bus to celebrate the second world title. RELATED: Argentina Welcomes National Squad with a Fans Massive Caravan After World Cup 2026. Celebrations continued Monday with receptions at Zarzuela Palace and Moncloa Palace, followed by players and coaching staff changing into commemorative shirts for the open-top bus tour, interacting with crowds through dances and songs.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: Spain 1-0 Argentina (AET): Ferran Torres' extra-time winner secures Spain's second title
news.lede: Spain won their second World Cup title after Ferran Torres' 106th-minute smashed finish earned a 1-0 win against defending champions Argentina in the 2026 final. Spain were deserved winners. Torres scored with Spain's 20th shot while the game ended without Argentina registering a single shot on target. Lionel Messi touched the ball just 54 times in a match that took three hours to complete and featured a 27-minute half-time break.

news.title: “Showing My Husband What Real Football Is”: Travis Hunter’s Wife Teases Jaguars Star at FIFA World Cup Final
news.lede: Travis Hunter has spent the offseason proving he can do just about everything on a football field. But according to his wife, Leanna Lenee, there’s still one version of football he needs to be educated in. While attending the FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina, Lenee couldn’t resist taking a playful jab at the Jacksonville Jaguars star. Lenee joked in a recent Instagram post.
